---
title: "KJV Isaiah Example"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
source("notebooks/00_source_project.R")
```

## 1. Load or prepare section documents

```{r load-prepare}
prepared_path <- "data/prepared/kjv_section_docs.csv"

if(file.exists(prepared_path)){
  section_docs <- readr::read_csv(prepared_path, show_col_types = FALSE)
} else {
  kjv <- readr::read_csv("data/raw/kjv.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)
  section_docs <- prepare_kjv_sections(kjv)
  dir.create("data/prepared", recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
  readr::write_csv(section_docs, prepared_path)
}

section_docs %>%
  dplyr::select(doc_id, section, macro_genre, n_verses, n_chapters, books)
```

## 2. Run default non-POS ensemble

POS requires `udpipe` and an English model. This version is fast and good for first testing.

```{r run-default-no-pos}
config_no_pos <- default_config(
  include = c("char", "word", "stem", "function_words", "punctuation", "sentence", "lexical")
)

result_no_pos <- run_stylometry_ensemble(section_docs, config_no_pos, k_neighbors = 8)
round(result_no_pos$overall_similarity, 3)
```

## 3. Isaiah rows only

```{r isaiah-rows}
isaiah_docs <- rownames(result_no_pos$overall_similarity)[grepl("Isaiah", rownames(result_no_pos$overall_similarity))]
round(result_no_pos$overall_similarity[isaiah_docs, , drop = FALSE], 3)
```

## 4. Class-level similarities

```{r class-sims}
names(result_no_pos$class_similarity)
round(result_no_pos$class_similarity$char, 3)
round(result_no_pos$class_similarity$function_words, 3)
```

## 5. Export results

```{r export}
export_ensemble_results(result_no_pos, output_dir = "output")
```

## 6. Optional POS run

Uncomment this chunk after installing `udpipe`.

```{r optional-pos, eval=FALSE}
# install.packages("udpipe")
config_with_pos <- default_config(
  include = c("char", "word", "stem", "function_words", "pos", "punctuation", "sentence", "lexical")
)

result_with_pos <- run_stylometry_ensemble(section_docs, config_with_pos, k_neighbors = 8)
round(result_with_pos$overall_similarity, 3)
```
